CITY OF OAKLAND

CITY HALL • ONE CITY HALL PLAZA, • OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612
(415) 273-3601
Office of the City Attorney TTY 839-6451
Jayne W. Williams
City Attorney October 27, 1992

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804

Re: Reply to Your October 6, 1992 Response to Our
September 14, 1992 Request for Advice, Government
Code section 83114(b) - Councilmember Frank Ogawa

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Thank you for your prompt response to our September 14,
1992 request for legal assistant regarding a potential conflict of
interest of Councilmember Frank Ogawa.

In reply to your October 6, 1992 response, enclosed
please find a copy of a letter from Max A. Morris dated October 23,
1992, in which he addresses the areas of concern outlined at page
3 of your October 6, 1992 letter to me. In sum Mr. Morris states:

1. Although Councilman Ogawa's house is
within 2,500 feet of the proposed project,
Councilman Ogawa's property will not be
affected because of the topography, downslope
and trees.

2. The Ogawa property is a single family
home in a single family neighborhood and it
does not have any development potential. If
the home was rented (to produce income) that
income would not change due to the building or
not building of the Dunsmuir Heights Project.

3. The Ogawa property is located on a street
that will not be affected by traffic, noise
levels, air emissions, intensity of use or
other traits that may result from the proposed
Dunsmuir Project.

[illegible] TO PTSD

[Most of the page appears to be blank or extremely faded, with only a few barely legible fragments visible in the middle section that are too unclear to transcribe with confidence]

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
October 27, 1992
Page 2

     I trust that this third letter from Mr. Morris will
provide the Commission with sufficient information to determine
whether it is advisable to rely on Mr. Morris' appraisal that the
Dunemuir Project will not have a material financial effect on
Councilmember Ogawa's property.

     Please advise me at your earliest convenience of your
determination.  If we can provide you with further information,
please do not hesitate to contact us.


                                        Very truly yours,

                                        JAYNE W. WILLIAMS
                                        City Attorney

                                        By: Joyce M. Hicks
                                            Assistant City Attorney


cc: Councilmember Frank Ogawa
Enclosure

H:\JWH\CONFLICT\OGAFPPC.L2

OCT 24 '92 14:56 TO CITY COUNCIL OFFICE FROM ADAMS ADAMS MORRIS T-534 P.02

ADAMS • ADAMS-
& MORRIS INC.

3541 Jamison Way, Suite 130, Castro Valley, CA 94546
(510) 537-4314


October 23, 1992


Mr. Frank Ogawa
Oakland City Council
11041 Ettrick Street
Oakland, CA 94605

RE: Disputing The Possibility Of A Conflict Of Interest
     When Voting As A City Council Member On A Potential
     Development Site Near Councilman's Place Of Residence

Dear Councilman Ogawa:

As I have stated in my previous correspondence with you it
is my firm belief that any decision by the City of Oakland
as to the approval or denial of the Dunsmuir Heights
Project will not affect the value of your home in any way,
positive or negative. This statement is meant to include
changes in marketability, quiet enjoyment and market
value.

The California Fair Political Practices Commission has
stated in a letter to your office that I had not taken
their regulation 18702.3(D) into consideration in
determining that your property value would not be affected
either in a positive or a negative manner.

As a matter of fact I had requested, received, and
reviewed Reg. 18702.3(D) along with other pertinent
regulations prior to writing my first letter to you (July
10, 1992).

To put this matter to rest I will attempt to address the
three main issues of Reg. 18702.3(D) as outlined in the
subject commission letter to you.

Item #1 Reg. 18702.3(D)
     1. The proximity of the property which is the
        subject of the decision and the magnitude of the
        proposed project or change in use in relationship
        to the property in which the official has an
        interest.

My reply: Although Councilman Ogawa's house is within
2,500 feet of the proposed project, Councilman Ogawa's
property will not be affected because of the topography,
downslope and trees.

Residential • Commercial • Investment Real Estate • Property Management

Page 2
October 23,1992

2. Whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will affect the development potential or
income producing potential of the property;

My reply: The Ogawa property is a single family home in a
single family neighborhood and it does not have any
development potential. If the home was rented (to produce
income) that income would not change due to the building
or not building of the Dunsmuir Heights Project.

3. Whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will result in a change to the character
of the neighborhood including, but not limited to,
the effect on traffic, view, privacy, intensity of
use, noise levels, air emissions, or similar traits
of the neighborhood.

My reply: The Ogawa property is located on a street that
will not be affected by traffic, noises levels, air
emissions, intensity of use or other traits that my result
from the proposed Dunsmuir Project.

Again, due to the topography, hills, slope and trees the
proposed Project will not affect Councilman Ogawa's view,
privacy or quality of enjoyment in either a positive or
negative manner.

Sincerely,

Max A. Morris

MAM:ey

11-12-1992 09:37AM FROM OAKLAND CITY ATTORNEY TO 9191632720226 P.02

Brian Kangas Foulk

November 6, 1992

Honorable Frank Ogawa
Oakland City Hall
606 14th Street
Oakland, CA 94607

Dear Councilman Ogawa:

We checked the distance from the closest boundary line for the Dunsmuir Heights property to the closest boundary line of your house at 11041 Ettrick Street, Oakland.

We used three references to make the measurements. They were the Thomas Brothers Maps for Alameda County, the U.S. Geological Survey Quadrangle Maps, and we also used the Alameda County Assessor's pages.

The distance we arrived at varied between approximately 2100 feet and 2200 feet. We can use a figure of 2150 feet, plus or minus 50 feet. We trust that this meets your need.

Very truly yours,
BRIAN KANGAS FOULK

[Signature] Mitchell L. Moughon
Mitchell L. Moughon
Registered Civil Engineer No. 28509

MLM:der

[Professional seal image]

Professional Services Since 1915

Geyserville Business
23670 Korbel Blvd.
Suite 300
Healdsburg, CA 95473
707/433-1576
Fax 707/433-6249

#3/P. 3:1:95

TOTAL P.02
